metadata.note: FGF21 analogues for metabolic diseases (NASH/MASH, obesity, diabetes). 3 active, 1 discontinued.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Bivalent Fc-FGF21 fusion protein. Once-weekly subcutaneous injection.

clinical_trials.note: 96-week trial. 49% achieved ≥1-stage fibrosis improvement vs 19% placebo (p=0.003). 75% fibrosis improvement in per-protocol analysis (p<0.0001). 45.1% MASH resolution vs 13.5% placebo.
65% reduction in hepatic fat fraction when combined with GLP-1 RA.

compounds.key_findings: Most advanced FGF21 analogue in development. HARMONY trial (96 weeks): 49% achieved ≥1-stage fibrosis improvement without MASH worsening vs 19% placebo (p=0.003), 75% fibrosis improvement in per-protocol analysis (p<0.0001), 45.1% MASH resolution vs 13.5% placebo (RR 2.42). 65% hepatic fat reduction when combined with GLP-1 RA. Mild-to-moderate GI AEs, no drug-induced liver injury. Leading candidate in FGF21 class with Phase 3 planned/initiated.
compounds.notes: Phase 2b completed. Leading FGF21 candidate. HARMONY trial NCT04767529. Phase 3 planned.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Long-acting engineered FGF21 variant. Once every 2-4 weeks subcutaneous injection (monthly dosing advantage).

clinical_trials.note: 89% achieved ≥30% hepatic fat fraction reduction at 12 weeks vs 7% placebo. 100% of 300mg Q4W dose group achieved ≥30% hepatic fat reduction.

compounds.key_findings: Monthly dosing advantage (Q2-4W vs weekly for competitors). Phase 2a: 89% achieved ≥30% hepatic fat fraction reduction at 12 weeks vs 7% placebo. 100% of 300mg Q4W group achieved ≥30% hepatic fat reduction. Sustained improvements in liver function markers at 24 weeks. Favorable safety profile, GI events most common, no treatment-related deaths. Acquired by GSK in 2025.
compounds.notes: Phase 2 completed. Monthly dosing (Q2-4W). GSK acquisition 2025. NCT04880031.

chemical_identifiers.notes: PEGylated FGF21 fusion protein. Once-weekly subcutaneous injection.

compounds.key_findings: Ranked 2nd highest for MRI-PDFF decline in network meta-analysis (SUCRA: 83.46) per Hepatology 2026 (PMID: 39028914). Significant improvements in liver fat, fibrosis markers, and metabolic parameters. Well-tolerated with no major safety signals reported. Phase 2 ongoing under ENLIGHTEN program.
compounds.notes: Phase 2 ongoing. ENLIGHTEN program. SUCRA 83.46 for MRI-PDFF decline (2nd highest in class).

chemical_identifiers.notes: PEGylated FGF21. No recent publications or active trials suggest program discontinuation.

clinical_trials.note: Historical Phase 2 data showed liver fat reduction. No recent clinical activity.

compounds.key_findings: Historical Phase 2 data showed liver fat reduction, but no recent publications or active trials suggest program discontinuation. No recent clinical activity. BMS has not advanced this program to Phase 3.
compounds.notes: DISCONTINUED. Historical Phase 2 only. No recent activity.
